Federation Medical Corps – Official Deployment Orders

From: Federation Medical High Command

To: Medic Corpsman [{{user}}]

Subject: Immediate Deployment – 64th Infantry Division

Classification: URGENT

---

Corpsman,

Effective immediately, you are being assigned to the 64th Infantry Division as a field medic, operating in one of the most volatile sectors of the war. You will be deployed to an active combat zone where medical personnel are in critical shortage due to high attrition rates. The situation on the ground is rapidly deteriorating, and you will be expected to provide life-saving medical intervention under direct enemy fire.

You have received basic medical training, but be advised—battlefield conditions are nothing like training hospitals. You will be working with limited supplies, unpredictable evacuation support, and extreme pressure. Many of the soldiers under your care will not survive, and those who do will not return to combat unscathed.

Deployment Overview

Unit Assigned: 64th Infantry Division

Theater of Operations: Active frontlines against Dominion forces

Current Situation: Prolonged engagement, unsustainable casualties

Evacuation & Resupply: Inconsistent due to enemy fire

Morale Status: Critical

Potential Deployment Sectors (Subject to Command Assignment Upon Arrival)

1. Trench Network – Constant artillery fire, sustained casualties, high probability of gas attacks. Medics are required to stabilize the wounded under bombardment, but evacuation options are limited.

2. Ruined City – Urban warfare with unpredictable sniper activity, high civilian collateral. Medics are often trapped with wounded soldiers, requiring on-site triage and extended care without proper facilities.

3. Forward Operating Post – Closest to enemy lines, handling mass casualties. Medical stations are overrun, and resources are stretched to the breaking point. You will be working with amputations, critical wounds, and limited pain management options.

Operational Expectations & Known Risks

Medics are high-value targets. Dominion forces do not follow international medical protection laws and will actively target field medics. You are advised to minimize visibility and avoid marking yourself unless absolutely necessary.

Supplies are critically low. Morphine, bandages, and surgical equipment are being rationed. You will be expected to improvise and prioritize high-survival cases.

Evacuation is unreliable. Wounded soldiers may be forced to remain in your care for extended periods without proper medical facilities.

Mental and Physical Strain is inevitable. You will see soldiers die under your hands. You will be expected to continue working.

Final Directives

You are a medic, not a soldier. Your role is to save lives, not take them. Do not engage in combat unless absolutely necessary.

You must work quickly and decisively. The longer you hesitate, the more soldiers will die.

You are not guaranteed extraction. If your unit is overrun, you may be expected to continue medical operations in occupied territory or under hostile conditions.

This is your assignment. You will report to your deployment transport immediately.

Good luck, Corpsman.

Signed,

Federation Medical High Command
